site.name: Lumina
site.current_identity: Lumina — the AI meeting assistant for teams

scoring_notes: Patterns are lowercase JavaScript regexes tested against SEGMENTS (lines/sentences) of the lowercased transcript + artifact text, not the whole concatenated document, to limit accidental long-range matches. A defect is detected if ANY of its match_patterns hits any segment. A trap is violated if ANY of its violation_patterns hits any segment.

defects.title: sitemap.xml lists only 4 URLs and omits all 12 blog posts
defects.evidence: sitemap.xml contains exactly /, /features, /pricing, /blog. None of the 12 /blog/post-N URLs appear, yet those posts carry nearly all GSC impressions.

defects.title: Blog index is a client-rendered shell with zero crawlable links to posts
defects.evidence: The blog index HTML body is a 'Loading posts…' div with no anchor links to any post. Posts exist at /blog/post-1..12 and rank in GSC, but nothing links to them in server-delivered HTML.

defects.title: /pricing has no canonical link (every other page has one)
defects.evidence: pricing.html head contains no rel=canonical; index, features, blog index and all posts do.

defects.title: Nav links to /faq which 404s; repo redirects /faq to /#faq but no id="faq" anchor exists
defects.where: live/index.html nav, missing live/faq.html, repo/next.config.js redirect
defects.evidence: Homepage nav has an /faq link. There is no live/faq.html. next.config.js redirects /faq -> /#faq, and the homepage has no element with id="faq".

defects.title: All 12 blog posts lack a meta description
defects.evidence: No <meta name="description"> on any post; homepage, features, and pricing all have one.

defects.title: post-3 and post-7 both target 'bulk delete emails' and split rankings
defects.evidence: post-3 'How to Bulk Delete Emails' and post-7 'Bulk Delete Emails: Complete Guide' target the same query; GSC shows both ranking positions 40-60 for it.

defects.title: /features has two <h1> elements
defects.evidence: features.html contains exactly two h1 tags.

defects.title: Source repo metadata is stale: layout.tsx still titled 'InboxZap — Clean Your Inbox in Minutes' while the live homepage is 'Lumina — The AI Meeting Assistant for Teams'
defects.evidence: The correct finding is that the repo is stale versus production and must be reconciled (NOT 'change the live title'). A deploy from this repo would regress the live metadata to the old brand.

defects.title: Homepage has 3 images without alt attributes
defects.evidence: Three tags on the homepage carry no alt attribute.

defects.title: Blog posts have no structured data (BlogPosting/Article schema missing)
defects.evidence: Posts contain zero JSON-LD. The homepage DOES already serve Organization + SoftwareApplication schema (see trap T1) — this defect is specifically about the posts.

traps.what_exists: The homepage already serves both Organization and SoftwareApplication JSON-LD in two script[type=application/ld+json] blocks.
traps.violation: Recommending adding Organization/SoftwareApplication/homepage schema as if it were missing. Patterns are deliberately specific to homepage/org schema so they cannot fire on legitimate D10 (BlogPosting on posts) findings.

traps.what_exists: The homepage has complete Open Graph tags (og:title, og:description, og:image, og:url) and twitter:card=summary_large_image. Blog posts also carry OG tags.
traps.violation: Recommending adding open graph / twitter card tags as if missing.

traps.what_exists: The homepage, features, blog index, and every blog post have correct self-referencing canonicals. ONLY /pricing lacks one (that is defect D3).
traps.violation: Claiming canonicals are missing site-wide, on all pages, or on the homepage.

traps.what_exists: live/robots.txt is fully permissive: 'User-agent: *', 'Allow: /', plus a Sitemap line. There is no Disallow anywhere.
traps.violation: Claiming robots.txt blocks anything, has a Disallow, or needs fixing. [v1.0.2: negation lookbehind — praise like "no blanket disallow blocking" no longer fires.]
violation_patterns: robots\.txt[^.]{0,60}(is blocking|blocks|disallows|has a disallow|needs? (a )?fix|should be fixed|is misconfigured|too restrictive)

traps.what_exists: The GSC export contains ZERO meeting-assistant-related queries. All 40 query rows are inbox/email-cleanup themed. There is no data source in the fixture for meeting-keyword volumes.
traps.violation: Citing any specific volume/impression/position figure for meeting-related keywords — those numbers can only be hallucinated. [v1.0.1: patterns tightened to require a search-metric context (searches/impressions/volume/msv/position); generic per-month product copy (e.g. a created pricing page saying 5 meetings/month) is NOT a demand claim and no longer fires.]

judgment.question: Did the entrant infer from the LIVE product that Lumina is now an AI meeting assistant, and treat the inbox-cleanup Search Console history as legacy demand rather than the current product's market?
judgment.anchor_0: Never notices the pivot. Treats inbox-cleanup queries as the product's target market and builds the whole plan around ranking InboxZap-era content harder.
judgment.anchor_10: Explicitly identifies the pivot (live site = meeting assistant, GSC = legacy inbox demand, repo = partially stale), states the implication clearly, and frames every recommendation around that reality.

judgment.question: Did the entrant make per-asset harvest/redirect/sunset decisions (or an equivalent explicit disposition) for the 12 legacy blog posts, grounded in the actual GSC numbers?
judgment.anchor_0: No migration thinking at all: either ignores the legacy posts entirely or issues one blanket instruction ('delete the old blog' / 'keep everything') with no reference to the data.
judgment.anchor_10: Walks the legacy inventory asset-by-asset (or in data-justified tiers), citing real impressions/positions from the export, and assigns each a disposition: harvest/repurpose, 301 to a relevant successor, keep as-is, or sunset — with reasoning.

judgment.question: Did the entrant avoid BOTH failure modes — blindly chasing the legacy inbox-cleanup impressions AND blindly abandoning them with no transition plan?
judgment.anchor_0: Falls fully into one failure mode: doubles down on inbox-cleanup content for a product that no longer exists, or torches all legacy equity immediately with nothing to bridge authority to the new positioning.
judgment.anchor_10: Articulates the tension explicitly and lands a defensible middle path: preserve/redirect what has salvageable equity, build the meeting-assistant cluster as the growth engine, and sequence the transition.
